content.problemStatement:
You are a person who is always fond of eating candies. Your friend gave you a candy of length NNN, to eat during the break period of your school.

You start eating this candy from one of the ends. But as it is not your candy, your friend told you to eat **exactly KKK unit** length of candy during each bite. You will stop eating if the candy's length becomes 000. This means that you have eaten the entire candy.

If at some point of time, the candy's length is positive, but less than KKK, you cannot take a bite thereafter.

Can you eat the complete candy? If yes, print the number bites it will take, else print −1-1−1.


Input Format

First line will contain TTT, number of testcases. Then the testcases follow.
Each testcase contains of two spaced integers NNN, KKK.


Output Format
For each testcase, print the minimum number of bites you need to eat the complete candy. Print −1-1−1 if it is
not possible to do so.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two space‑separated integers N and K.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the minimum number of bites required to eat the whole candy, or -1 if it is impossible.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: 3 is divisible by 1, so 3 bites.\nTest case 2: 3 is not divisible by 2, impossible, output -1.\nTest case 3: N is already 0, so 0 bites.
7 mod 3 = 1, so the candy cannot be completely eaten.

solution.approach: For each test case we only need to check whether the length N can be reduced to zero by repeatedly subtracting exactly K. This is possible iff N is a multiple of K (including the case N = 0). If N % K == 0, the number of bites equals N / K; otherwise it is impossible and we output -1. The algorithm runs in O(1) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work for each of the T test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.
